metadata.title: UAVSig Dataset Readme
metadata.abstract: The UAVSig dataset was collected to develop drone and remote controller identification using RF fingerprinting. This dataset includes captures from multiple drones and controllers, with more detail about the experimental setup found in our paper [1]. The following files are provided:

secondary_disciplines.level1: 电子、通信与自动控制技术

evidence.original_text: The UAVSig dataset was collected to develop drone and remote controller identification using RF fingerprinting. This dataset includes captures from multiple drones and controllers, with more detail about the experimental setup found in our paper [1]. The following files are provided: (1) Data files (*.mat files): 192 controller files, 96 one-drone files and 432 two-drone files. (2) A readme file: readme.pdf. (3) A MATLAB script to visualize the .mat files: visualize.m. (4) A Python script to crop the signals and generate spectrograms: generate_spectrogram.ipynb. (5) A Python script to automate the downloading of the dataset: dataverse_download.py. If you use UAVSig dataset/code or any (modified) part of them, please cite our paper [1].

(5) A Python script to automate the downloading of the dataset: dataverse_download.py. If you use UAVSig dataset/code or any (modified) part of them, please cite our paper [1].

The UAVSig dataset was collected to develop drone and remote controller identification using RF fingerprinting. This dataset includes captures from multiple drones and controllers, with more detail about the experimental setup found in our paper [1]. The following files are provided: (1) Data files (*.mat files): 192 controller files, 96 one-drone files and 432 two-drone files. (2) A readme file: readme.pdf. (3) A MATLAB script to visualize the .mat files: visualize.m. (4) A Python script to crop the signals and generate spectrograms: generate_spectrogram.ipynb. (5) A Python script to automate the downloading of the dataset: dataverse_download.py. If you use UAVSig dataset/code or any (modified) part of them, please cite our paper [1].

Each loaded data file contains the raw I/Q data as well as some meta data for the labels: data - 1x50e6 complex matrix of the raw I/Q samples bw - List of DSP estimated bandwidths for each bounding box fc - List of center frequencies of each bounding box start, end - Lists of start and end sample indices respectively for each bounding box id - List of transmitter labels for each bounding box num_transmission - Total number of transmissions found in the data.

entries.description: MATLAB data file format containing raw I/Q samples and metadata labels

evidence.original_text: Controller data: controller_p _p1_  s1s2s3s_ 4 f1f2_ .mat _p1_ : an integer in (1, 2), representing the position (spatial placement) of the controllers. _s1_ -4: a binary code in (0, 1), representing the status of a controller. 0 stands for off and 1 stands for on. _f1_ -2: an integer in (0, 1, ..., 9), representing the index of the file. The index starts from 00 and the maximum is 05. Example: `controller_p1_1010_02.mat` : position 1, controllers 1 and 3 are on, the third capture. Drone data: drone c1c2c3c_ 4 f1f2_ .mat _c1_ -4: an integer in (0, 1, 2, 3, 4), representing the channel of a drone with the corresponding index and 0 stands for an off status. i.e., _c1_ stands for the channel of drone 1. _f1_ -2: the integer in (0, 1, ..., 9), representing the index of the file. The index starts from 00 and the maximum is 05. Example: `drone_2300_00.mat` : drone 1 on channel 2, drone 2 on channel 3, drones 3 off, drone 4 off, the first capture.

entries.description: File naming scheme for controller and drone data files

evidence.original_text: The UAVSig dataset was collected to develop drone and remote controller identification using RF fingerprinting. This dataset includes captures from multiple drones and controllers, with more detail about the experimental setup found in our paper [1]. The following files are provided: (1) Data files (*.mat files): 192 controller files, 96 one-drone files and 432 two-drone files. (2) A readme file: readme.pdf. (3) A MATLAB script to visualize the .mat files: visualize.m. (4) A Python script to crop the signals and generate spectrograms: generate_spectrogram.ipynb. (5) A Python script to automate the downloading of the dataset: dataverse_download.py. If you use UAVSig dataset/code or any (modified) part of them, please cite our paper [1].
